sources.title: Profiles in Courage - Governor John M. Slaton - Leo Frank Case - Colorized 2021
sources.description: Hollywood's JFK Profiles in Courage (1964) is about Georgia's very corrupt 60th Governor John M. Slaton (1913-1915) and his relation to the rape-murder trial of Leo Frank. This crooked politician is rehabilitated in this odious drama, while the White Gentile Southerners are smeared as a hideous mob of torch wielding yahoos. Leo Frank was calmly hanged August 17th, 1915 at a former Sheriff's farming estate, William Frey. Leo Frank the toilet strangler was a Jewish supremacist and habitual pedophile born at Cuero Texas in 1884 and he ran a pencil producing sweatshop in Atlanta Georgia from 1908 to 1913. He was arrested, indicted by a Coroner's jury, Indicted and convicted for the molestation-murder of one of his child laborites Mary Phagan. 20 factory girl's at his Mary Phagan murder trial, testified about his pedophilic character for lasciviousness, saying it was bad. The drama is an anti-Gentile Hollywood film written and produced by the Jewish Supremacists in the media of the 1960s who falsify the facts and evidence of the Leo Frank case to smear the Southerners who convicted the homicidal Jewish serial pedophile Leo Frank for the rape and murder of 13-year-old Mary Phagan. This 1964 Profiles in Courage agitprop, is a Hollywood drama that rehabilitates the corrupt 60th governor of Georgia, John Slaton, who commuted the death sentence of his law firm's criminal defense client Leo Frank to life in prison over his 1913 conviction for the rape-murder of 13-year old Mary Phagan.

metadata.Plot: With a bent toward inspiring even the direst cynic, COURAGE: Profiles in Creativity takes a close-up look at the courage needed to transform the ordinary into the extraordinary. Through the work of 20 unique individuals including corporate presidents, painters, rap musicians, and scientists, the film takes us on a powerful journey into the very essence of creativity. It examines what creativity is, how people get started, its' role in our society, the link between creativity and spirituality, and personality traits of the highly creative. With the candor of actor John Lithgow, the wisdom of author John Gardner, and the academic expertise of Harvard Professor Teresa Amabile, we discover what makes for an optimum creative environment and how to get back to our instinctual roots. Conversely, COURAGE explores the effects of creativity gone awry. A thought-provoking and humorous journey from precarious beginnings to full scale accomplishments, COURAGE inspires audiences to dust off the old paintbrush and unlock their own distinct creativity. Additional individuals interviewed include Director Bille August, Opera Diva Suzanna Guzman, World Champion runner Lynn Jennings, crop artist Stan Herd, whose canvases are fields around the globe which can only be properly viewed from the air; Jimmy Palmiotti and Joe Quesada, creators of the comic book "Ash," the basis for a forthcoming Dreamworks feature animated film; Lead singer/songwriter of "Spearhead," Michael Franti; award-winning scientist and MIT professor, Alexander Rich; Apple Computer Designer Robert Brunner; Principal dancers of Buglisi/Foreman Dance, as well as other individuals.
metadata.Actors: Bille August, Michael Franti, Guzman Suzanna
